mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects sustained consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy raises corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and accelerating consumer prices creates restrictive real monetary conditions that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median and CPIAUCSL is accelerating month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when unemployment is below 5.5%, month-over-month growth in industrial production signals resource constraints and rising input costs that squeeze corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5% and INDPRO is rising month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for materials and credit, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% over 3 months coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and collapsing consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 2 months coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of retail sales and industrial production confirms a demand-supply expansion loop where consumption growth validates production investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO are rising month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the effective federal funds rate tighten liquidity conditions and raise short-term discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to rapid repricing.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of observations.
